title: Traveling with Kyle - Living in Chile Part 7/17

transcript.text: Yeah, it's like a two-bedroom house one bedroom one small bedroom connected to another small bedroom with a door
And then there's one bathroom one small kitchen and then like a living room and that's the house and
Then there's this area where all these people would park their cars
And that's I guess how they made most of their money is just people who would pay them to park their cars back
There because people are afraid to park their cars out on the street because people would
Smash the windows in and steal the cars like my wife's best friend's car was stolen that way
So now she has to pay somebody to park their car her car and behind somebody's gate and
So
They had this area back there, but they could oh they couldn't park many cars though because
Because along the whole side for like a hundred feet at least it was just a pile of
Uh of just crap like
Eight ten foot high pile of crap and
Uh
But anyways, yeah, he's sitting in my mini and uh so then the brother the older brother comes over and I hear him like
Yelling at him and stuff so I peek around the corner just to see what's going on and he's like god
He's on top of him his father with his hand around his throat like punching him in the face
Like what'd you do and hit my mama so then I run up to him and I grab his arm and I'm like whoa hold on don't be punching your dad in the face
So he gets off of him
So then the dad gets up and runs over and picks up a brick and is about to smash his head over the head with it
But he didn't notice that by then the cops had been there so the cops basically were standing right behind him
So they grabbed his arm
And they put him in shackles and took him off to jail and he was there for like two months
So when he was in jail
See I was kind of planning on uh getting rid of all that trash, but he wanted me to pay him
To get rid of the trash because he thought that he could sell the trash anyways
So now he and I actually did pay him or a little bit
And I was gonna pay him or something, but anyways, he was thrown in jail. So I was like, oh cool
That means I can get rid of all this stuff. So
I hired pickups and dump trucks to come over and pick up all the trash and it was
I think in the end I counted like 25 pickup loads full of trash
Because it was all along the side of the house and then behind the house there was this area where there was this car
And then piled up on top of the car and next to the car like eight feet high was trash
And it was sketchy because it was like, you know, there were like spiders back there and it wasn't a safe place
So I got rid of all of that and then I kind of got addicted to kind of doing that
I was doing a couple of private classes, but by then I wasn't working at this service at the irs building anymore because
The company the institute I was working for I like tried to get legal with them
And they kept writing up a
Um
Contract for me
To take into the immigration, but they kept writing it wrong and I ended up going to immigration like six times
And every time I went there they had something else wrong about the contract and it sucked because I had to wait like two hours in line
Every time I went there
And then they finally just fired me
So I guess what they really wanted to do is just not hire me legally and they just couldn't tell me that so
You know when they realized I wasn't going to give up on that they just took my classes away
So then I wasn't I was kind of only working for myself and having money problems
But just then
My wife's best friend where she works out of town
There was this lady this Chilean lady who has her own institute. So she calls me up and offers me jobs
So I take some classes she gave me and I got she was complaining that
She had english teachers native english teachers, but they kept quitting on her because the class was like
It was like
It was like ooh like 10 miles out of town or something way way way up north of town like right before where the toll
Station is to go in
Um
And I was like oh cool because that meant that I could like actually ride my bike out there
It was like a reason for me to ride
And I really liked you know having a reason to ride a long ways to get a class because uh
Because the way that I was eating you know down there. It's like lots of fried uh fried pig called chuleta
It's fried and that's what like my girlfriend would cook for me. And so I would eat that a lot and
And then there's another delicacy there called uh
Uh
Well, it's like it's like it's like vegetables mixed together and kind of a vegetable salad, but the thing the
Compound that makes it all stick together is mayonnaise
So we're eating lots of mayonnaise and there's even this other thing that she'd make that's like a hollowed out tomato
Filled with mayonnaise mixed with uh
Tuna but it's mostly mayonnaise man and like yeah really bad diet dude like I'm gross that was funny
I was at my house once and my wife gives one to my girl my little her son
And my friend was there he's going oh, I bet you he's just gonna eat the mayonnaise and not even eat the take the tomato
And that's sure enough he did that he scoops all the mayonnaise out eats it and then leaves the tomato
But yeah, so it's kind of hard plus plus she's like everybody there especially my wife. They're all addicted to coca-cola
So I was drinking that too
So I was like developing this really big belly like if I pinch my belly it'd be like two two and a half to three inches thick of just fat
You know and I'd flex my stomach and I'd see like a little bit of rivets and I'd be like, okay
And I like pull my stomach down. Okay, I guess I still have kind of a six pack
But I was starting to get fat
It's like so anything like reason to ride my bike
I guess since now I'm on the subject I'll talk about Chilean food Chilean food
Is the worst food anywhere I've ever been because it's not healthy because of those two dishes I said but then they have this other dish called uh
Calde pollo or something
And what that is is it's a bowl
Of like boiled water
And inside of that you have a chicken leg or a chicken breast
But it's totally like it's just a chicken breast no seasoning thrown in there boiled
With the flesh like coming off of the bones, so it's kind of like you're eating a dead animal
And then they have a corn cob thrown in there
And then like a couple of vegetables in the bottom
So if you eat it
You have to wait like 20 minutes because it's all boiling hot and you can't just you can't eat the corn
With fork and knife so you have to wait till that cools down then you eat that with your mouth
And then you can't eat the eat the broth because it's just salt water with no side seasonings and then there's like the chicken that you eat
So that's one and then there's this other thing called chuleta
Um
No, no, no called churrasco
Which is a really thin slice of beef
But it's like it's like roadkill beef
Um the problem with like like cows from argentina are good they have good meat in argentina because
The cows eat really well lots of grass and they all live on flat plains most of argentina is perfectly flat plains like australia
And um
Actually come to think of it that's one austral one one argentinian told me that that's why they had one of the reasons they had money problems
It's because there was floods we were taking a train through going into
Buenos Aires and he's like look floods there was just like floods so deep that the cars were like all the wheels all the way up to the
Tops of the wheel wells were filled with water and
I guess that meant that the cows couldn't eat as much meat so they couldn't export as many cows and that's why they had their problem
Couldn't pay off their loans, but anyways
They have good meat but in chile all of their cows are like on these big ranges up on the sides of mountains
You know running up and down mountains, so they're all like buff like a marathon runner
And then I guess I've heard from people who know meat that they don't know how to cook them cut their media
They'll just chop the meat off and then just use whatever slab of meat or something
But anyways, and then they don't
Refrigerate it right, but like yeah, you get this stuff called a
Churrasco and it's just this thin slice of beef
That smells bad it even smells like it's rotten
And if you take a bite of it even if you like saw with your teeth
And you squeeze the bread you can't separate it from the rest of it
It pulls the whole thing out so then you have to like pull it and tear it free from your mouth
It's like you're a wolf or something
And then by then the bread has totally crumbled into nothing
So then you just have to finish it off with your hands or a fork and knife and so that's like why when I was in
Santiago every time I went out to eat I just went to mcdonald's because the other thing is
They overcharge for churrasco they charge more for just one churrasco sandwich than for a whole mcdonald's meal
And then you have to wait like 20 minutes to get it because they never have the oven on
They have to turn the oven on get the oven heated
Before they can throw their churrasco in to cook it and stuff so it's just way better to just go to mcdonald's
But um
And then they have this other let's see what else do they eat
Um the fish market's pretty good though for like a dollar or something really cheap or a dollar and a half at the time
And you guys dollar the dollar was like 750 pesos to the dollar when I was there when I was having money problems
So that was good right now. It's like 400 or something
So it's a thousand pesos at the time you could go and get this uh fish
It's like a whole fish there and then a pile of like vegetables or something and it was really good
We used to go there a lot with my girlfriend
But uh yeah, so but anyways yeah, she cooked out food and I was kind of starting to get fat
So it was cool to ride along so I was like cool hook me up
I'll do those jobs those drives those jobs those teach classes
And it usually took me like 45 minutes once I got stronger to get out there and there was like it was like on a frontage road along the highway, so
